name: Tiny Fruit Turnovers
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Jo Anne Merrill
Preparation Time: 1:00
1. Sift together flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, beat sugar, margarine, eggs, and lemon peel with an electric mixer on low to medium speed until light and fluffy. Gradually add the flour mixture until well combined and smooth. Form into a ball of dough and wrap tightly. Refrigerate for several hours or overnight.
3. Make the filling before continuing with the cookie dough. In a small saucepan, combine chopped dried apricots, apricot nectar, sugar, and 1/2 cup water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to simmer and cook, covered, for 15 minutes. Add chopped dates and cook an additional 5 minutes, stirring frequently to ensure the fruit doesn't dry out or stick. Remove from heat and stir in lemon peel, juice, and nuts. Mix well and allow to cool completely.
4. Divide dough into four parts and work with one part at a time, refrigerating the remaining dough until ready. On a lightly floured surface, roll the dough into a rectangle measuring 10 x 12 inches. Cut into 12 rectangles using a sharp knife or cookie cutter.
5. Place cookies onto a lightly greased baking sheet. Spread about 1 teaspoon of filling on half of each piece and fold over to seal the edges firmly with a floured fork. Prick the tops in one or two places. Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 10-12 minutes, until lightly browned. Do not overcook. Repeat with remaining dough, greasing the baking sheet as needed.
Yield: Approximately 48 turnovers.
